title: Simultaneous EEG and fNIRS recordings for semantic decoding of imagined animals and tools
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous EEG and fNIRS recordings from 12 participants performing semantic imagery tasks involving silent naming and sensory-based imagination of animals and tools using visual, auditory, and tactile modalities. EEG data were acquired using a 64-channel BioSemi system at 2048 Hz, while fNIRS data were collected with a NIRx NIRScoutXP system using frontal and temporal montages. The dataset supports research in semantic brain-computer interfaces and multimodal neuroimaging analysis.
methods_description: EEG was acquired using a BioSemi ActiveTwo system with 64 electrodes positioned according to the 10-20 system, plus bilateral earlobe reference electrodes, galvanic skin response measurement, and respiration monitoring at 2048 Hz sampling rate. fNIRS data were collected simultaneously using a NIRx NIRScoutXP continuous wave imaging system with 8 light sources and 4 detectors in integrated fNIRS-EEG optode caps with frontal and temporal montages.
